target_launch_segment.segment_name: Art students at large urban universities in Egypt
target_launch_segment.why_first: Highest pain intensity, most reachable via university partnerships and Instagram, and strongest word-of-mouth potential within tight campus communities. Concentrated geographically — easier to serve and support in early weeks.
target_launch_segment.size_estimate: ~80,000 art students across Cairo, Alexandria, and Giza universities

positioning_message.headline: Quality art supplies. Delivered locally. From verified sellers you can trust.
positioning_message.subheadline: No more waiting 3 weeks for international shipping or settling for low-quality local options.
proof_points: Every seller verified by our team before going live
Fawry and Mada payments — no international card needed
WhatsApp support that responds within 1 hour
positioning_message.tone: Warm, community-first, practical — not corporate or luxury
positioning_message.arabic_headline: لوازم فنية أصيلة، توصيل محلي، من بائعين موثوقين.

marketing_channels.role: Primary brand and product discovery channel
High-conversion direct channel for early adopters
marketing_channels.target_metric: 30 DMs per week, 15% conversion to first purchase

marketing_channels.role: Referral channel with built-in trust and community
marketing_channels.target_metric: 2 signed partnerships by end of month 1, 20 referral orders from each

marketing_channels.role: Scale channel after organic proves messaging

funnel_stages.description: Target customer discovers the platform exists
funnel_stages.key_action: Follows Instagram or clicks profile link
funnel_stages.metric: Instagram impressions + link-in-bio clicks per week

funnel_stages.description: Potential buyer explores the catalogue and seller profiles
funnel_stages.key_action: Browses products, views seller profile, reads reviews
funnel_stages.metric: Catalogue page views, avg session duration > 90 seconds

funnel_stages.key_action: Adds to cart → selects payment → confirms order

funnel_stages.description: Buyer returns for a second purchase within 30 days
funnel_stages.key_action: Opens re-engagement message and clicks back to marketplace

funnel_stages.description: Satisfied buyer refers a friend or university peer
funnel_stages.key_action: Shares referral link or organic word-of-mouth recommendation
funnel_stages.metric: Referral orders as % of total orders

launch_experiments.name: University DM blitz
launch_experiments.hypothesis: Sending personalised WhatsApp messages to 50 art students in week 1 will generate 8+ orders
launch_experiments.method: Identify 50 art students on Instagram. Send a short personal message with a first-order discount. Track who orders.

launch_experiments.name: Instagram reel product showcase
launch_experiments.hypothesis: A 30-second reel showing the unboxing of a verified art supply order gets 3%+ link-in-bio click rate
launch_experiments.method: Post 3 product reels in week 2. Use a tracked link-in-bio URL. Measure clicks per reel.

launch_experiments.name: University club exclusive discount
launch_experiments.hypothesis: Offering a university art club a 10% group discount will generate 15+ orders from their members
launch_experiments.method: Partner with 1 university art club. Give their members an exclusive code. Track redemptions.
launch_experiments.success_metric: 15+ code redemptions within 2 weeks of partnership

launch_experiments.name: First paid Instagram ad
launch_experiments.hypothesis: A $50 Instagram ad targeting Cairo 18-30 art interests gets 20+ clicks at < $2.50 CPC
launch_experiments.method: Run 1 Instagram ad for 5 days at $10/day. Target Cairo, interests: art, painting, design. Single product showcase.

weekly_breakdown.primary_action: 50 WhatsApp DMs to art students + 5 seller onboarding sessions
University art club partnerships (2 clubs) + Instagram reel launch
First $50 Instagram ad + WhatsApp re-engagement of week 1-4 buyers
Referral programme launch (give $5 credit for each referred friend who orders)

key_actions: Build a list of 200 art students on Instagram before week 1
Prepare 3 seller product videos before launch
Write 5 personalised DM templates for different buyer types
Sign 2 university club partnerships in week 1
Set up a simple WhatsApp broadcast list for weekly new arrivals

launch_timeline.focus: Seller setup complete. First 5 sellers live. 50 WhatsApp DMs sent.
3 Instagram reels posted. University outreach started.
First university club partnership signed. Exclusive discount live.
WhatsApp re-engagement of all previous buyers. 2nd university club.
First $50 Instagram ad. Referral programme announced.
Onboard 3 more sellers based on demand gaps. Feature 5 products.
Referral programme fully live. Weekly new arrivals broadcast.
Review all metrics. Decide which channel to double down on.

kill_metrics: Fewer than 15 orders by end of week 4 — messaging or channel is wrong
Add-to-cart → purchase conversion below 3% after 50 catalogue visits
More than 3 sellers request to leave in first 6 weeks

cac_tracking.tracking_method: UTM parameters on all links + manual order source tagging in Notion
cac_tracking.weekly_review: Every Monday: calculate orders per channel vs spend. Double down on channels with CAC < $10.
cac_tracking.blended_cac_formula: Total marketing spend / total new customers acquired in the same period

feedback_loops.method: WhatsApp voice note request — 'Can you send me a 30-second voice note about your order?'
feedback_loops.what_to_capture: Product quality, delivery speed, payment experience, overall satisfaction
feedback_loops.action: Log to Notion. Fix any issue mentioned by 2+ buyers within 1 week.

feedback_loops.method: WhatsApp message: 'Hey [name], what would make you order again?'
feedback_loops.what_to_capture: Price sensitivity, product range gaps, platform friction
feedback_loops.action: Add top 3 requested product categories to seller onboarding wishlist

feedback_loops.what_to_capture: Platform ease of use, order volume satisfaction, payment timing, support quality
feedback_loops.action: Fix any process issues. Use feedback to improve seller onboarding guide.

summary: The GTM strategy is community-first and manual for weeks 1-8: WhatsApp DMs, university partnerships, and Instagram organic content — all at under $150 total spend. Paid Instagram ads enter only in week 5 after organic messaging is validated. Four experiments in weeks 1-5 progressively test channel viability before any budget commitment. CAC is tracked per channel from day 1 using UTM tags and Notion, with a Monday review ritual. The three kill metrics tell the founder when to pivot channel strategy rather than continuing to invest in what isn't working.
